key_insight: TFEM stabilizes finite element computations on degenerate tetrahedral meshes by capping the effect of near-zero Jacobians, preventing sliver-induced locking while preserving meaningful solution modes.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a practical solver-side alternative to expensive sliver removal, enabling reliable 3D simulations on imperfect meshes in CFD, phase-field, wave, and vibro-acoustic problems.
method: Introduce a lower bound on the Jacobian determinant for degenerate tetrahedra.
Interpret the correction as a vanishing added-volume term that limits singular stiffness contributions.
Preserve physically relevant modes while suppressing artificial constraints from sliver clusters and sheets.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on representative problems, where TFEM consistently recovers accurate, physically meaningful solutions in cases where standard FEM shows locking or loss of convergence.
watch_item: The approach changes the effective element volume in degenerate regions, so the threshold choice must be tuned carefully to avoid over-regularizing legitimate geometric features.
paper_title: Taming Slivers: A Robust TFEM Framework for Reliable Computations on Degenerate Tetrahedral Meshes
